title: Passage
description: One passage from the D21 minimal passage index: the unit that axis (c) faithfulness verdicts anchor on. This schema describes a SINGLE passage record; commands that emit lists (`passages search`, `passages index`) validate each element against it. The passage_id is hash(document content hash + section path + character offsets + parser version), so it is stable across machines and across re-extraction as long as the document bytes and the parser version are unchanged, and it intentionally CHANGES on a parser upgrade, which is what makes verdicts replayable per D15. The field set mirrors the FTS5 passages table: passage_id, doc_hash, section_path, char_start, char_end, page_coords, passage_hash, text.

passage_id.description: Stable passage identifier: hash(doc_hash + section_path + char_start + char_end + parser_version) per D21.

doc_hash.description: Content hash of the extracted canonical text of the whole document. Stored per passage so the M3 compiler can detect stale evidence when a source PDF is re-fetched and differs.

passage_hash.description: Content hash of this passage's text alone. Lets a consumer detect that a passage changed even when the document hash is unavailable.

section_path.description: Hierarchical section path from the heuristic section split, most general first, joined with '/' (for example 'Methods/Participants'). The empty string is permitted for text that falls outside any detected heading, since it must still hash to a stable ID.

char_start.description: Inclusive character offset of the passage in the document's canonical extracted text.

char_end.description: Exclusive character offset of the passage in the document's canonical extracted text. Always greater than char_start.

page_coords.description: Page rectangles this passage occupies in the source PDF, in PDF user-space points. Null (or an empty array) for HTML sources, which have no pagination; extraction never invents coordinates it does not have.

text.description: The passage text, exactly as extracted and indexed. This is what BM25 ranks over.

doc_id.description: Identifier of the indexed document, canonically the lowercased DOI. Not part of the passage ID hash.

parser_version.description: Parser version that produced this passage. Recorded because it is an input to passage_id: the same bytes under a different parser version yield a different ID by design.

ordinal.description: Zero-based position of the passage within the document, in reading order.

bm25_score.description: BM25 rank score for the query, present only in `passages search` output. Lower is better under SQLite FTS5 bm25(), which returns a negated score; the CLI reports the raw FTS5 value. Comparable only within one query.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 content hash, hex encoded, optionally prefixed with 'sha256:'.

pageCoords.description: Page rectangles, origin at the top left of the page, coordinates in points.

page.description: One-based page number in the source PDF.
